name: dsd-heroku

metadata.name: dsd-heroku
metadata.summary: A plugin for django-simple-deploy, supporting deployments to Heroku.

documentation:
# dsd-heroku

A plugin for deploying Django projects to Heroku, using django-simple-deploy.

For full documentation, see the documentation for [django-simple-deploy](https://django-simple-deploy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/).

modules.name: dsd_heroku.deploy_messages
documentation: A collection of messages used in deploy_heroku.py.

functions.name: dsd_heroku.deploy_messages.success_msg
documentation: Success message, when not using --automate-all flag.
--- Dynamic strings ---
These need to be generated in functions, to display information that's
determined as the script runs.

functions.name: dsd_heroku.deploy_messages.success_msg_automate_all
documentation: Success message, when using --automate-all.

modules.name: dsd_heroku.deploy
documentation: Manages all Heroku-specific aspects of the deployment process.

functions.name: dsd_heroku.deploy.simple_deploy_automate_all_supported
documentation: Specify whether --automate-all is supported on the specified platform.

functions.name: dsd_heroku.deploy.simple_deploy_get_automate_all_msg
documentation: Get platform-specific confirmation message for --automate-all flag.

functions.name: dsd_heroku.deploy.simple_deploy_get_platform_name
documentation:
Return the name of the platform that's being deployed to.

DEV: Consider returning a config object if there ends up being more info to share
back to core.

functions.name: dsd_heroku.deploy.simple_deploy_deploy
documentation: Carry out platform-specific deployment steps.

modules.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer
documentation: Manages all Heroku-specific aspects of the deployment process.

classes.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer.__init__
documentation: Establishes connection to existing simple_deploy command object.

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer.deploy
documentation: --- Public methods ---

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._validate_platform
documentation:
Make sure the local environment and project supports deployment to Heroku.

Returns:
    None

Raises:
    SimpleDeployCommandError: If we find any reason deployment won't work.
--- Helper methods for deploy() ---

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._handle_poetry
documentation:
Respond appropriately if the local project uses Poetry.

If the project uses Poetry, generate a requirements.txt file, and override the
initial value of sd_config.pkg_manager.

Heroku doesn't work directly with Poetry, so we need to generate a
requirements.txt file for the user, which we can then add requirements to. We
should inform the user about this, as they may be used to just working with
Poetry's requirements specification files.

This should probably be addressed in the success message as well, and in the
summary file. They will need to update the requirements.txt file whenever they
install additional packages.

Note that there may be a better way to approach this, such as adding
requirements to Poetry files before generating the requirements.txt file for
Heroku. It's not good to have a requirements.txt file that doesn't match what
Poetry sees in the project.

See Issue 31:
https://github.com/ehmatthes/django-simple-deploy/issues/31#issuecomment-973147728

Returns:
    None

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._prep_automate_all
documentation:
Do intial work for automating entire process.
- Create a heroku app to deploy to.
- Create a Heroku Postgres database.

Sets:
    str: self.heroku_app_name

Returns:
    None

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._ensure_db
documentation:
Ensure a db is available, or create one.

Returns:
    None

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._add_requirements
documentation: Add Heroku-specific requirements.

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._set_env_vars
documentation: Set Heroku-specific environment variables.

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._add_procfile
documentation: Add Procfile to project.

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._add_static_file_directory
documentation: Create a folder for static files, if it doesn't already exist.

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._modify_settings
documentation:
Add Heroku-specific settings.

This settings block is currently the same for all users. The ALLOWED_HOSTS
setting should be customized.

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._conclude_automate_all
documentation: Finish automating the push to Heroku.

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._summarize_deployment
documentation:
Manage all tasks related to generating and showing the friendly
summary of the deployment.

This does not take the place of the platform's official documentation.
  Instead, it gives the user a friendly entry into the platform's
  official documentation. It also gives them a brief summary of some
  followup steps they can take, for example making a second push, or
  changing the URL of the deployed app.

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._show_success_message
documentation: After a successful run, show a message about what to do next.

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._check_heroku_settings
documentation: Check to see if a Heroku settings block already exists.
--- Utility methods ---

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._check_cli_installed
documentation:
Verify the Heroku CLI is installed on the user's system.

Returns:
    None

Raises:
    SimpleDeployCommandError: If CLI not installed.

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._check_cli_authenticated
documentation:
Verify the user has authenticated with the CLI.

Returns:
    None

Raises:
    SimpleDeployCommandError: If the user has not been authenticated.

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._check_heroku_project_available
documentation:
Verify that a Heroku project is available to push to.

Assume the user has already run `heroku create.`

Returns:
    None

Raises:
    SimpleDeployCommandError: If there's no app to push to.

Sets:
    dict: self.apps_list
    str: self.heroku_app_name

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._create_postgres_db
documentation:
Create a Heroku Postgres database.

Returns:
    None

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._set_heroku_env_var
documentation:
Set a config var to indicate when we're in the Heroku environment.
This is mostly used to modify settings for the deployed project.

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._set_debug_env_var
documentation: Use an env var to manage DEBUG setting, and set to False.

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._set_secret_key_env_var
documentation: Use an env var to manage the secret key.

methods.name: dsd_heroku.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._generate_summary
documentation: Generate the friendly summary, which is html for now.

Perform the initial deployment to Heroku.

If --automate-all is used, carry out an actual deployment.
If not, do all configuration work so the user only has to commit changes, and run
`git push heroku main`.
